Source code for src.utils.google_oauth_flow

"""Google OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow helpers (Authlib + OpenID userinfo)."""

from __future__ import annotations

import urllib.parse
from datetime import datetime, timezone

import requests
from authlib.integrations.requests_client import OAuth2Session, OAuthError
from authlib.oauth2.rfc6749.errors import OAuth2Error
from fastapi import HTTPException, status
from sqlalchemy import func
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from src.config import settings
from src.models.user import User

GOOGLE_AUTH_URL = "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
GOOGLE_TOKEN_URL = "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
GOOGLE_USERINFO_URL = "https://openidconnect.googleapis.com/v1/userinfo"




[docs]
def get_google_redirect_uri() -> str:
    """
    Single source of truth for Google OAuth redirect_uri.
    Must be identical in: authorize URL (/o/oauth2/v2/auth), token POST (/token),
    and Google Cloud Console → Authorized redirect URIs.
    """
    return settings.GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI




def _assert_authorize_url_redirect_matches(auth_url: str) -> None:
    """Ensure the built authorize URL embeds the same redirect_uri we use in fetch_token."""
    expected = get_google_redirect_uri()
    parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(auth_url)
    qs = urllib.parse.parse_qs(parsed.query)
    raw = (qs.get("redirect_uri") or [None])[0]
    if not raw:
        raise ValueError("Google authorize URL is missing redirect_uri")
    got = urllib.parse.unquote(raw)
    if got != expected:
        raise ValueError(
            f"authorize redirect_uri mismatch: URL has {got!r}, expected {expected!r}"
        )




[docs]
def normalize_email(email: str) -> str:
    return email.strip().lower()






[docs]
def oauth_session() -> OAuth2Session:
    return OAuth2Session(
        client_id=settings.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
        client_secret=settings.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET,
        redirect_uri=get_google_redirect_uri(),
    )






[docs]
def create_google_authorization_url(state_jwt: str) -> str:
    """Build Google authorize URL; state is our signed JWT for CSRF."""
    client = oauth_session()
    extra: dict = {"scope": "openid email profile"}
    if settings.GOOGLE_OAUTH_ACCESS_TYPE_OFFLINE:
        extra["access_type"] = "offline"
    if settings.GOOGLE_OAUTH_PROMPT_CONSENT:
        extra["prompt"] = "consent"
    # Explicit redirect_uri so it matches fetch_token() and Google Cloud Console exactly.
    url, _ = client.create_authorization_url(
        GOOGLE_AUTH_URL,
        state=state_jwt,
        redirect_uri=get_google_redirect_uri(),
        **extra,
    )
    _assert_authorize_url_redirect_matches(url)
    return url






[docs]
def exchange_code_for_token(code: str) -> dict:
    """
    Exchange authorization code for tokens.
    Authlib uses HTTP Basic auth for client credentials by default (not body + Basic).
    """
    client = oauth_session()
    code = urllib.parse.unquote(code.strip())
    return client.fetch_token(
        GOOGLE_TOKEN_URL,
        code=code,
        redirect_uri=get_google_redirect_uri(),
    )






[docs]
def fetch_openid_userinfo(access_token: str) -> dict:
    r = requests.get(
        GOOGLE_USERINFO_URL,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}"},
        timeout=30,
    )
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.json()






[docs]
def derive_display_name(userinfo: dict, email: str) -> str:
    name = userinfo.get("name") or userinfo.get("given_name")
    if name:
        return name[:150]
    local = email.split("@", 1)[0]
    return local[:150]






[docs]
def resolve_google_user(
    db: Session,
    *,
    google_sub: str,
    email_raw: str,
    full_name: str,
) -> User:
    """
    Resolve an existing user by Google subject or email (link first-time Google to a local row).

    Does not create new users; unknown Google identities get a clear error.
    """
    email_norm = normalize_email(email_raw)
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    user = db.query(User).filter(User.google_id == google_sub).first()
    if user:
        if not user.is_active:
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
                detail="Account is inactive",
            )
        user.last_login_at = now
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(user)
        return user

    matches = (
        db.query(User).filter(func.lower(User.email) == email_norm).all()
    )

    for u in matches:
        if u.google_id is None:
            u.google_id = google_sub
            if u.auth_provider == "local" and not u.password_hash:
                u.auth_provider = "google"
            if u.email_verified_at is None:
                u.email_verified_at = now
            u.last_login_at = now
            if not u.is_active:
                db.rollback()
                raise HTTPException(
                    status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
                    detail="Account is inactive",
                )
            db.commit()
            db.refresh(u)
            return u

    for u in matches:
        if u.google_id is not None:
            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
                detail="This email is already linked to a different Google account",
            )

    raise HTTPException(
        status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
        detail=(
            "No account exists for this Google sign-in. "
            "You must be invited or registered before you can sign in."
        ),
    )






[docs]
def map_oauth2_error(exc: OAuth2Error | OAuthError) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Return (user_message, log_detail). Covers RFC6749 OAuth2Error and Authlib OAuth2Session OAuthError."""
    err = getattr(exc, "error", None) or ""
    desc = getattr(exc, "description", None) or str(exc)
    combined = f"{err} {desc}".lower()
    if err == "invalid_grant" or "invalid_grant" in combined:
        return (
            "Authorization code is invalid, expired, or already used. "
            "Google allows one exchange per code—if the callback ran twice "
            "(e.g. React Strict Mode) the second request fails; start sign-in again "
            "and ensure the callback only exchanges the code once.",
            desc,
        )
    if "redirect_uri_mismatch" in combined:
        return (
            "redirect_uri_mismatch: the URI used when exchanging the code must be identical "
            "to the one in the authorize step and in Google Cloud Console (scheme, host, port, path, no trailing slash). "
            "Use GET /auth/google/start and open auth_url; do not hand-build the Google URL unless redirect_uri matches "
            f"GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI exactly (server currently uses {get_google_redirect_uri()!r}).",
            desc,
        )
    return ("Google sign-in failed. Please try again.", desc)
